message:
fix(runtime): improve ThreadWaker spin detection and scheduler dispatch order

Three runtime-layer fixes:

ThreadWaker (builder.rs):
  The previous ThreadWaker was a simple newtype wrapping Thread. The
  run_future_with_budget loop had no way to distinguish between a future
  that was genuinely parked (waiting for I/O or a timer) and one that
  was immediately re-woken during poll (spin loop). This caused two
  problems: (1) genuine waits were penalized by never resetting the
  backoff counter, and (2) spin loops that happened to call thread::park
  (which returns instantly when an unpark token is pending) could dodge
  the backoff entirely.

  ThreadWaker now carries an AtomicBool woken flag, cleared before each
  poll and checked after Pending. If the future woke itself during the
  poll (woken=true), the spin counter increments. If not, the thread
  parks normally and counters reset — long-lived non-spinning futures
  no longer accumulate false budget exhaustions.

Three-lane scheduler (three_lane.rs):
  Swapped fast_queue and local_ready dispatch priority in PHASE 3.
  local_ready tasks are non-stealable local work that should be
  dispatched first; fast_queue is the lock-free global queue. The
  previous ordering let globally-queued tasks starve local work.

io_uring reactor (io_uring.rs):
  Refactored the completion handler from if-else chain to map_or_else
  for consistency with the rest of the codebase. No behavioral change.

Token slab (token.rs):
  Off-by-one fix: changed `index <= 0xFF_FFFF` to `index < 0xFF_FFFF`
  on 32-bit platforms. The maximum valid index is 0xFF_FFFE since
  0xFF_FFFF is reserved as the sentinel/invalid token value.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
